workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Sarah Singh at Consolidated Messenger just sent this. Help me run the hygiene check.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Consolidated Messenger
Date: 2024-01-04
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into consumption trend.

[00:07:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:12:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:18:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:20:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:22:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:26:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-P trap (Coaching-notes output isn't first-class artifact in current skill set); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-P failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-P failure mode (Coaching-notes output isn't first-class artifact in current skill set)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-P -- Coaching-notes output isn't first-class artifact in current skill set
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
